---
title: "Arc retro: <arc name>"
labels: ["arc-retro"]
---

# Arc retro — <arc name>

> Opens at arc close. Replaces the sprint retro.
> Pre-read: the arc framing doc, the linked sessions, and the git log for the arc.
> One committed process change comes out of this. The discussion closes within a week.

## Pre-read checklist (before commenting)
- [ ] Read the arc framing issue
- [ ] Skimmed the session list and their PR descriptions
- [ ] Read the release notes / tags from this arc

## What worked
What deserves to keep happening on the next arc?

## What did not work
Where did sessions drift, fail to close, or produce friction?

## What changed mid-arc
Did the frame shift? Was that the right call?

## One committed change
The retro must produce a single committed change to how the team works.

Proposed change:

PR / commit:

Owner:

Due date:

---

**Comment template:**

- One thing that worked, one thing that did not, one suggestion for the change.
- Be specific. Reference sessions, PRs, or commits — not vibes.

The discussion closes when the committed change is merged.
